focus: Stress-test reducer, registry, dashboard, and report persistence: how null-search evidence, search debt, ruled-out candidates, and clean-path proof should survive synthesis.
summary: Reducer/dashboard/report persistence is the fragile link: current reducer state is finding-centric, dashboard verdicts are severity-centric, ruled-out prose collapses clean proof with dead ends, report synthesis lacks a Search Ledger destination, and validation does not prove reducer survivability.

graphEvents.label: Reducer drops ledger-shaped evidence unless promoted into registry state
Dashboard PASS can hide search debt
Ruled-out persistence collapses clean proof and dead ends

label: Search-ledger rows would currently be dropped by the reducer unless they are promoted into explicit reducer state
recommendation: Add reducer-owned candidateCoverage, searchDebt, ruledOutCandidates, searchCoverage, and cleanSearchProof fields before treating emitted searchLedger rows as durable.

label: The current dashboard can still render a clean PASS while hiding search debt
recommendation: Expose search debt in dashboard status, progress, and active risks; v2 non-trivial missing-search rows should affect PASS posture or set a blocking search-debt flag.

label: Existing ruled-out persistence collapses clean proof, dead ends, and rejected candidates into one prose bucket
recommendation: Separate ruledOutCandidates, cleanSearchProof, and blockedSearchDebt with row-level evidenceRefs and dispositions.

label: The report compiler has no first-class Search Ledger destination
recommendation: Add a reducer-backed Search Ledger/Search Coverage section to review-report synthesis before the appendix.

label: Post-dispatch validation currently proves artifact shape, not reducer survivability
recommendation: For v2 reviews, validate ledger row quality plus persistence parity: rows must be reducer-readable and dashboard/report-visible, not merely present.

label: Every ledger field must have a named persistence owner before validator enforcement becomes hard-error
Absence-of-finding claims must be as auditable as active findings in registry, dashboard, and report output
The reducer loads flattened delta payloads before registry construction, so the correct insertion point is available but currently finding-only

direction: Use only existing Ruled Out markdown as search-ledger persistence
reason: It collapses clean proof and dead-end semantics and lacks row-level evidenceRefs/disposition preservation.

direction: Use graphEvents as the only candidate-search persistence path
reason: graphEvents are optional in the iteration prompt and graphless runs need equivalent text/JSON fallback proof.
